text: Finally something I actually understand. These big companies betting on nuclear makes me want to add some CEG to the college fund.
author: mom_of_3_trading

text: CEG getting crushed while signing the biggest deals in their history. Market never makes sense in the short term.
author: sharp315

text: Nuclear is coming back hard and these big tech deals prove it. Wish I grabbed more CEG when it was lower.
author: fomo_king_420

text: This is exactly when you buy. Sentiment is trash but the fundamentals just got way better. Classic contrarian setup.
author: whohebe123

text: solid play. nuclear is the real clean energy story nobody gives credit for.
author: ragnaroksunset

text: My cousin is a cop and he says Axon cameras are literally everywhere in their department. Had no idea it was public.
author: noob_investor42

text: Worked with their evidence.com platform. The switching costs are real once a city integrates everything. Sticky as it gets.
author: work_from_zoom

text: Dropped 50% you say? Guess who bought right before that happened. Youre welcome everyone.
author: bought_at_the_top

text: The subscription model is the moat. Once a PD signs up for evidence.com the renewal rates are absurdly high.
author: data_dependent_1

text: 50% off a company that prints cash is a gift if you can hold for a couple years. Check back in 2028.
author: CCWaterBug

text: Cerebras makes those giant single chips right? This feels like buying into something early. At least stocks cant rug pull.
author: crypto_refugee_88

text: The margin miss was just accounting noise from switching to direct sales. Revenue growth is still intact and WSE-3 is legit.
author: turned_into_a_newt

text: Halved from IPO price?? Is this a buying opportunity or am I about to catch a falling knife? Talk me off the ledge.
author: help_me_pls77

text: Corrections like this are normal for new tech IPOs. The hardware is actually impressive and they have real customers.
author: Latter-Possibility

text: Buying the dip on a stock thats already halved? Name a more iconic duo. I respect it though.
author: whats_a_stop_loss

text: My father in law has been telling me to buy RTX for years. Record backlog and they make Patriot missiles. Hard to argue with that.
author: grillmaster_finance

text: Just doing my regular buy and RTX keeps showing up in the cart. Defense contracts are basically guaranteed revenue for years.
author: paycheck2paycheck

text: Would RTX be a good first stock for someone just starting out? I want something that feels safe and defense seems like it isnt going anywhere.
author: first_time_caller99

text: NATO hitting 3%+ spending targets changes the revenue picture for years. This is bigger than any single quarter print.
author: macro_dad_energy

text: Safe is relative but defense contractors have pretty locked in revenue. Just dont go all in at once. DCA over time.
author: RN_Geo

text: Been holding AMD since 2021. Finally feels like they might actually have something to challenge the big guy. About time.
author: still_holding_2021

text: Night shift checking in. AMD looking interesting with the MI400 rumors. Could get spicy in premarket.
author: night_shift_trader

text: Been watching AMD for a while now. Is the MI400 actually the real deal or are we getting more hype?
author: just_lurking_69

text: Seen some unusual call activity piling up on AMD lately. Someone with deep pockets might know something about MI400.
author: option_flow_watcher

text: rocket labs vertical integration is the real story here. they build everything in house and the margins are starting to show it. neutron rocket is the next catalyst.
author: grillmaster_finance

text: just opened a small position in RKLB. space stocks seem like the future. hope i didnt buy at the wrong time lol
author: first_time_caller99

text: backlog growth is what caught my attention. theyre not dependent on a single customer like some other space names. diversified revenue stream is the moat here.
author: data_dependent_1

text: i work in networking and can confirm arista is the default in every hyperscaler data center. cisco is playing catch up and the gap is widening every quarter.
author: work_from_zoom

text: got some ANET after reading about their switches. still trying to figure out the difference between them and cisco honestly. learning as i go.
author: noob_investor42

text: anet has been a core holding for years. they just keep executing quarter after quarter. the 1.6 terabit platform is going to power the next wave of ai buildout.
author: CCWaterBug

text: ge vernova is the boring infrastructure play that actually makes sense. transformers and turbines have multi year lead times and theyre essentially the only option at scale.
author: sharp315

text: bought GEV right after the spin off and watched it drop immediately. classic. but the ai power demand thesis is solid so im just sitting on it.
author: bought_at_the_top

text: the electrification backlog tells you everything you need to know. ai cant run without power and this company literally builds the grid. rates coming down is the cherry on top.
author: macro_dad_energy

text: cybersecurity is a must spend category but im cautious on s valuation. need to see better margins and faster path to profitability before i add more here.
author: RN_Geo

text: just bought sentinelone and already second guessing. crowdstrike seems way bigger and more established. should i be worried about this pick?
author: help_me_pls77

text: thesis is simple: purple ai is the differentiator. crowdstrike has brand advantage but sentinelones autonomous response tech is genuinely ahead. market will realize eventually.
author: thesis_driven_dan

text: stock down 36% after the biggest nuclear news in decades. microsoft meta and walmart all signed deals and the market yawned. this is a textbook mispricing.
author: Latrodectus1990

text: bought a few shares of CEG for the kids college fund. nuclear is making a comeback and i like owning things that produce power for decades.
author: mom_of_3_trading

text: ceg is one of those positions i just let sit and forget. microsoft and meta arent going to cancel those nuclear contracts. not checking this til december.
author: westcoastcouch

text: everyone talks about backlog but launch cadence is what actually matters. show me consistent monthly launches then ill get excited.
author: whohebe123

text: holding long term. space is a multi decade play.
author: ragnaroksunset

text: cisco still owns enterprise campus networking though. arista wins in cloud data center but theyre not the whole market.
author: CarrotAwesome

text: ai networking revenue doubled. this thing is just getting started. adding every dip.
author: whats_a_stop_loss

text: grid spending thesis is real but the valuation already prices in perfection. be careful paying up.
author: SheriffBartholomew

text: kicking myself for not buying more when it dipped. turbine backlog is absolutely stacked.
author: fomo_king_420

text: autonomous response sounds great until it false positives a critical system. id rather have human oversight.
author: Minimum-Criticism763

text: both can win. cyber is a huge market.
author: ragnaroksunset

text: just opened my first account and grabbed some CRWD. their AI security stuff sounds legit and my buddy who works in IT says everyone uses them now. hope i didnt buy at the peak lol
author: first_time_caller99

text: i like the CRWD story but ive been burned by high multiple growth stocks before. 97% renewal rate is insane though. gonna watch for a pullback before I add this to the portfolio.
author: RN_Geo

text: bought at the literal high and you are all welcome for the dip again. at least the 4-for-1 split makes my tiny position look bigger. still bullish long term honestly.
author: bought_at_the_top

text: so the argument against waiting is they literally power the SOC for half the fortune 500. 97% renewal with 80% margins isnt a story stock. the multiple compresses when rates drop.
author: CarrotAwesome

text: im in the same boat man. bought some CRWD and immediately started second guessing. but everyone keeps saying the AI moat is real so maybe we just hold through the noise
author: help_me_pls77

text: dumped my whole portfolio into META thinking open source AI was the future. now they go proprietary right after i bought in. my timing is a curse i swear
author: yolo_my_401k

text: everyone piling on meta for this pivot but thats exactly when you want to buy. sentiment is completely washed out and the ad business is still a cash machine. fading the hate.
author: whohebe123

text: saw META dropping and thought it was a sale. now im reading they killed llama and im confused. wasnt the whole point that open source was their edge? someone explain
author: noob_investor42

text: retail always gets sold a story and then the rug gets pulled. they hyped llama for years to juice the stock and now they flip. same playbook different ticker.
author: SheriffBartholomew

text: at least meta has real revenue unlike the garbage i was trading before. no gas fees. no rug pulls. just a company that prints money on ads. boring but way safer.
author: crypto_refugee_88

text: finally made an account after watching PLTR for months. the commercial side seems real this time not just govt contracts. curious what everyone thinks about the valuation though
author: just_lurking_69

text: my thesis on PLTR is dead simple. AIP turned their secret govt sauce into a commercial product and revenue is inflecting. 85% growth while the market sleeps is the exact setup.
author: thesis_driven_dan

text: my husband thinks im crazy for buying PLTR for the kids college fund since its already run so much. but their AI stuff seems different from the hype stocks. we use tech everyday so why not own it
author: mom_of_3_trading

text: been watching PLTR climb forever and kicking myself for not getting in earlier. the AIP demos look legit though. is it too late to start a position here or still room to run
author: fomo_king_420

text: just opened a roth ira and everyone says buy MSFT. ackman dumping alphabet for microsoft seems like a big deal for a new guy like me. is this a good time?
author: first_time_caller99

text: ackman rotating out of one mega cap into another tells you everything about the AI narrative reset. rates environment favors the strongest balance sheets and MSFT has the best one.
author: macro_dad_energy

text: been using microsoft products my whole life. office, teams, azure. my company literally cant function without them. bought some for the kids college fund.
author: grillmaster_finance

text: MSFT is about as safe as it gets for a first stock. just dollar cost average in. dont dump it all at once and youll be fine long term.
author: RN_Geo

text: yep
author: ragnaroksunset

text: night shift checking in. the ai inversion thing hits different when youre working a blue collar job. at least nobody is coming for the warehouse work anytime soon.
author: night_shift_trader
